title: Unreported NetSupport RAT C2 chain (mheusa04)
summary: A novel, unsubmitted NetSupport Manager RAT dropper staged on Aeza infrastructure delivers a fully unreported C2 chain. Procurement/tender-themed lure; financially-motivated (BEC/fraud/access) tradecraft against corporate finance teams.
detail: An open-directory sweep of Aeza staging infrastructure surfaced a native Windows loader (compiled ~2026-06-18, not present in VirusTotal). The loader carries a 4.9 MB encrypted overlay (magic 0x12345678) which decrypts with a static 8-byte XOR key to an embedded ZIP — a NetSupport Manager RAT package (PCICL32/HTCTL32/TCCTL32.DLL, remcmdstub.exe, Client32.ini). The recovered Client32.ini names a primary gateway (mheusa04[.]xyz:443) and secondary gateway (167[.]148[.]201[.]142:443), both fresh (~2026-06-09) and entirely unreported. The delivery domain mytenderdownload[.]com was VT-clean at time of discovery and serves a benign 86 KB procurement decoy PDF. The mheusaNN naming series (mheusa03 in April, mheusa04 in June) ties to a single operator via shared Dynadot registration and dyna-ns[.]net nameservers, NetSupport family reuse, and consistent business/procurement (PO/RFQ/tender) lures — hallmarks of a commodity BEC-ecosystem crew. Attribution beyond that pattern is low-confidence.
